title: erasanthe henrici subsp. henrici
intro: Erasanthe henrici subsp. henrici is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as a type of forget-me-not. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Erasanthe henrici subsp. henrici include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as providing relief from fever and other symptoms associated with infections and illnesses. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
